description: Prompt cost visibility and architectural documentation for the AI Gateway

architecture.principle: The gateway NEVER mutates prompt content. Prompt assembly is OpenClaw's domain.
gatewayRole: Route requests to the correct provider/model
Audit and log prompt token overhead per tier
Warn when overhead exceeds tier-appropriate thresholds
openclawRole: Assemble system prompt from workspace files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, USER.md, MEMORY.md)
Read PIC-synced data from workspace files at session start
Decide what context to include based on the query type

picSyncPipeline.description: PIC (Personal Identity Core) is a database service that syncs bidirectionally with OpenClaw workspace files

flow.picToWorkspace: PIC API → openclaw_memory_sync → USER_CONTEXT.md, pic_context.json → read by OpenClaw at session start
flow.keyPoint: PIC data is baked INTO workspace .md files via sync. The gateway sees normal system messages — not raw PIC.

tokenBudgetAudit.description: Observe-only thresholds. Gateway logs warnings but does NOT truncate or strip content.

sonnet.notes: Most expensive — flag bloated prompts for manual optimization

haiku.notes: Cheaper but still paid — flag large prompts

qwen.notes: Free local model — no budget concern

gemini.notes: 1M context, very cheap — no budget concern

futureOptimization.description: Prompt size reduction should happen at the SOURCE (OpenClaw workspace files), not in transit at the gateway
strategies: Trim SOUL.md: remove voice/heartbeat/group-chat sections if not needed for the session type
Trim AGENTS.md: move verbose heartbeat/cron docs to a separate reference file
OpenClaw tier awareness: if OpenClaw knows the target model, it can assemble a lighter prompt
Anthropic prompt caching: cache the system prompt to amortize cost across turns
